div.p: in the ability to perform repeated efforts. Alkalizing agents such as sodium bicarbonate (NaHCO 3 ) prevent large blood pH changes, however, there is no evidence on whether regulation of acid-base balance may also support three NaHCO NaHCO 3 and granulocytes, red blood cells count and haemoglobin concentration at post-exercise and 45-min recovery, and for white blood cells count, percentage of monocytes, concentration of magnesium and creatinine at 45-min 3 exercise or recovery shifts in studied blood indicators. div.head: INTRODUCTION
div.p: The high energy request of the organism during intensive efforts and the need for rapid anaerobic ATP resynthesis lead to the release of This occurs predominantly during high-intensity exercises and train- A,B,C and after plasma separation concentration of urea and creatinine, and Additionally, to avoid misinterpretation of blood parameter results due to different hydration status of participants on different visits, -Statistical Analysis ) and collection moment (rest, post-exercise and recovery), repeated -RESULTS The resting (rest) values of all haematological, biochemical and blood T Blood pH value -NaHCO 's, CTRL, PLA -
